channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Bluegrass rivalry enters new era with No. 5 Kentucky and Louisville led by Mark Pope and Pat Kelsey
item.description: LEXINGTON, Ky. (AP) — Transition remains for Mark Pope with No. 5 Kentucky and Pat Kelsey with Louisville, growing pains and all.
item.dc:creator: GARY B. GRAVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inspiring effort: De La Salle’s rally comes up short against Mater Dei in Open state title game
item.description: CIF Open Division state football championship 2024: De La Salle comes back from huge halftime deficit against Mater Dei, but big rally comes up short.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: Bill Belichick reached out to Jets about their vacancy before taking UNC job, AP source says
item.description: Bill Belichick contacted the New York Jets about their head coaching vacancy before accepting the job at North Carolina, according to a person familiar with the situation. The 72-year-old Belichick was introduced Thursday as North Carolina’s football coach.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chiefs cut kicker Matthew Wright days after game-winning field goal with Harrison Butker set to return
item.description: The Kansas City Chiefs cut kicker Matthew Wright after he made the game-winning field goal over the Los Angeles last week as Harrison Butker returns from injury.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 10-man Liverpool draws with Fulham in Premier League but Arsenal fails to take advantage
item.description: Premier League leader Liverpool twice came from behind with 10 men to draw with Fulham 2-2, yet dropped points for the second straight match to give hope to its title rivals on Saturday. Arsenal failed to take advantage.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pro Football Hall of Famer Randy Moss reveals cancer diagnosis, 6-hour surgery
item.description: Pro Football Hall of Fame wide receiver Randy Moss said Friday he is being treated for cancer and is recovering from major surgery. In an appearance on Instagram Live, Moss, aided by a cane, introduced himself as a cancer survivor and thanked his “prayer warriors” for their support.
item.dc:creator: ZACH ALLEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Game-worn Wilt Chamberlain Lakers jersey sells for $333K at auction
item.description: A game-worn Wilt Chamberlain jersey from the 1970-71 Los Angeles Lakers playoff run sold for $333,601 at Grey Flannel Auctions’ holiday auction this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Dick Vitale says he's cancer-free after 4 bouts with disease in just over 3 years
item.description: Vitale had surgery in the summer to remove cancerous lymph nodes from his neck. He was previously treated for melanoma and lymphoma, and had radiation treatments last year for vocal cord cancer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utah shuts down Colorado for fifth straight road victory
item.description: DENVER (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club is turning into a team of road warriors. Dylan Guenther scored two goals and an assist to give him a team-leading 12 goals on the season, while Karel Vejmelka made 22 saves, as Utah shut down the Colorado Avalanche Thursday night, 4-1.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: San Diego FC completes 2024 MLS Expansion Draft. Here is who they selected
item.description: San Diego FC's roster is starting to come together after taking part in the 2024 MLS Expansion Draft.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Bucs' run game faced with challenging front seven
item.description: Quarterback Baker Mayfield is faced with a challenging Chargers defense that is allowing the fewest points in the NFL. The Chargers are led by the edge-rush combo of Joey Bosa and Khalil Mack.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 29 CMU football student-athletes make RMAC All-Academic list
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—The Rocky Mountain Athletic Conference unveiled its All-Academic Team for football on Tuesday afternoon. Colorado Mesa University was well-represented, with 29 student-athletes receiving recognition, including five First Team honorees and one major award winner.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Ohio lawmaker proposes bill to make ‘flag planting’ in Ohio State stadium a felony
item.description: Days after the postgame melee between the Michigan and Ohio State football teams, an Ohio state lawmaker has introduced a new bill that would make “flag planting” within Ohio Stadium a felony criminal offense.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5

item.title: Cubs announce 2025 coaching staff
item.description: CHICAGO — The Cubs announced its 2025 coaching staff Monday under the helm of second-year manager Craig Counsell. There are four new additions to his staff, including replacements for third base coach Willie Harris and first base coach Mike Napoli.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WGN 9

item.title: Kyle Whittingham will return for 2025 season
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was a simple two word statement that made a resounding statement throughout the University of Utah athletic department. "I'm back." And with that, Utes head football coach Kyle Whittingham announced he will return to the program for his 21st season in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utah HC beats Flyers to sweep East Coast road trip
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (ABC4 Sports) - For the first time since the first week of the NHL season, the Utah Hockey Club has won back-to-back games, and they did it on back-to-back nights.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: LeBron James misses 1st game this season, sidelined versus Portland due to left foot soreness
item.description: LeBron James will sit out Sunday's game against the Portland Trail Blazers due to left foot soreness, the NBA career scoring leader's first missed game of the season.
item.dc:creator: DOUG PADILLA, Associated Press, Fox 5
